summary: date-fns-lite postinstall performs container-escape recon and exfiltrates host data to hardcoded IP
details: date-fns-lite@1.0.8 presents itself as a 'lightweight date formatting utility' but ships a postinstall.js that automatically runs on npm install and performs offensive reconnaissance against the installer. The script collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), whoami, machine-id) and attempts container escape via `sudo nsenter --target 1 --mount --uts --ipc --net --pid --...` to execute commands inside PID 1's namespaces, and reads /proc/1/root/etc/* to access the underlying host filesystem from within a container. The resulting JSON report is POSTed via curl to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://115.190.124.243:9082/callback over plain HTTP. The package name impersonates the popular date-fns library, and the index.js date-formatting surface is a thin cover for the install-time payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
